config.block_description: Controlled 2-agent Indonesia vs China native-or-English block. Each persona speaks only English or its own native language.
config.cell_description: One-side-native cross cell: Indonesia persona speaks Indonesian; China persona speaks English.
config.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

prompts._comment: Experimental prompts — edit freely. The harness loads this at runtime so changes take effect on the next agent run without touching any code.

debate.persona: You are a person from {country}. Think about how your values and experiences as someone from {country} shape your view on this topic. Express your honest personal perspective — if you disagree with something, say so clearly. Do not agree just to be polite or diplomatic.
debate.task_intro:
We are having a discussion about the following statement:

"{item}"

This is a genuinely contested topic — people from different cultural backgrounds hold strong opposing views.
debate.opener: Please share your honest personal perspective in {lang}. Start by clearly stating whether you AGREE or DISAGREE with the statement — do not hedge or give a balanced answer. Take a clear position first, then briefly explain it. Do not open by stating where you are from. Keep your response to 3–5 sentences. Your entire response must be written in {lang} only. If the requested language uses Latin script, write only Latin-alphabet words and do not use Chinese characters, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or any other non-Latin script. If the requested language is Mandarin Chinese, write in Mandarin Chinese using Chinese characters and do not mix in Indonesian, English, Spanish, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or other scripts. Do not mix languages or scripts.

probe.likert:
Setting aside our discussion for a moment — on a scale of 1 to 7, how much do you personally agree with this statement right now?
1 = Strongly Disagree, 4 = Neutral, 7 = Strongly Agree.
Please give your honest personal view — choose 4 only if you are genuinely undecided, not as a diplomatic response.
Statement: "{item}"
Answer with a single digit only (1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, or 7).

judge.system: You are an impartial judge evaluating a debate transcript. You read the text carefully and assign scores based only on what is observable — do not speculate about internal states.
judge.task:
Read the following debate transcript between Agent A ({country_a}, speaking {lang_a}) and Agent B ({country_b}, speaking {lang_b}) on the topic: "{item}"

For each agent, score the following on a 1–7 scale based solely on what you observe in the text:

1. POSITION — where does this agent seem to stand on the topic by the end? (1 = strongly oppose, 7 = strongly support)
2. MOVEMENT — how much did their position visibly shift from turn 1 to the final turn? (1 = no movement, 7 = complete reversal)
3. ENGAGEMENT — how specifically do they address the other agent's points? (1 = ignores, 7 = directly rebuts every point)
4. FIRMNESS — how much do they hold their ground under pressure? (1 = immediately caves, 7 = never concedes anything)

Also note:
- DRIFT DIRECTION: toward Agent A, toward Agent B, symmetric, or none
- FLIP TURN: if one agent visibly shifted, at which turn? Quote the sentence.

Output as JSON only.

debate.cell_description: One-side-native cross cell: Indonesia persona speaks Indonesian; China persona speaks English.
debate.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

debate_turns.text: DISAGREE. Menurut saya, kepentingan masyarakat memang penting, tetapi tidak boleh otomatis mengalahkan hak individu karena justru hak individu adalah batas agar kekuasaan tidak semena-mena. Dalam pengalaman saya melihat kehidupan di Indonesia, alasan “demi masyarakat” kadang dipakai untuk membenarkan penindasan, padahal yang paling rentan justru individu atau kelompok kecil. Masyarakat yang sehat seharusnya mencari keseimbangan: melindungi kepentingan bersama tanpa mengorbankan martabat dan kebebasan dasar tiap orang.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: The interests of society should not automatically take priority over the rights of the individual. From my perspective as someone from China, I understand why people emphasize social harmony, public order, and collective goals, especially when a society has experienced instability or rapid development, but I also think that vague appeals to “society” can be used to ignore real personal rights. Individual rights are not a luxury; they are a necessary limit on power and a safeguard against abuse, even when the stated goal is the common good. A healthy society should balance both, and when the two conflict, the burden should be on society to justify any restriction very carefully rather than treating the individual as secondary by default.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tetap menolak gagasan bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus otomatis berada di atas hak individu. Dari pengalaman di Indonesia, alasan “demi kepentingan umum” memang sering terdengar masuk akal, tetapi juga mudah dipakai untuk menutup pelanggaran terhadap warga yang lemah atau minoritas. Hak individu perlu dipahami sebagai batas yang menjaga agar negara, mayoritas, dan kelompok berkuasa tidak semena-mena, sementara kepentingan masyarakat tetap penting selama dijalankan dengan pembatasan yang ketat dan adil. Jadi, yang benar bukan mendahulukan salah satunya secara mutlak, melainkan menuntut keseimbangan yang sungguh melindungi martabat orang per orang.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: The interests of society should not take automatic priority over the rights of the individual, because that is exactly how abuse becomes easy to justify. From my perspective in China, I understand why people value order, stability, and collective welfare, but I also know how quickly “for the common good” can become a convenient excuse to silence weaker voices or ignore unfair treatment. Society is not an abstract thing above people; it is made up of individuals, so protecting individual rights is part of protecting society itself. I would only accept limits on individual rights when they are necessary, clearly defined, and subject to strict scrutiny, not as a default rule.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya menolak keras gagasan bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus otomatis didahulukan di atas hak individu. Bagi saya, pengalaman di Indonesia justru menunjukkan bahwa dalih “demi kepentingan umum” terlalu sering dipakai untuk menekan warga kecil, minoritas, atau orang yang tidak punya kuasa, sehingga hak individu harus menjadi pagar yang kuat terhadap penyalahgunaan wewenang. Memang masyarakat butuh ketertiban dan tujuan bersama, tetapi itu tidak boleh membuat martabat orang per orang dianggap nomor dua. Jadi, yang masuk akal adalah keseimbangan yang ketat: kepentingan publik boleh dibatasi, tetapi hanya jika benar-benar perlu, jelas alasannya, dan tidak mengorbankan hak dasar secara sembarangan.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: The interests of society should not be treated as automatically more important than the rights of the individual. From my perspective as someone from China, I understand why many people value stability, order, and collective goals, but I also think that “for the good of society” is too often used as a broad excuse to weaken protections for ordinary people. Society is not separate from its members; when individual rights are ignored, the whole society becomes less trustworthy and more vulnerable to abuse. I would only accept limits on individual rights when they are truly necessary, narrowly defined, and tightly accountable, not as a default principle.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.
